ogTitle: Stirling vs Stirling Range National Park: Climate Safety

heading: Stirling vs Stirling Range National Park — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Stirling, Western Australia and Stirling Range National Park, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Stirling Range National Park has a lower overall climate risk score (40/100) compared to Stirling (41/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Stirling or Stirling Range National Park safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Stirling has a flood risk score of 37/100, while Stirling Range National Park scores 42/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Stirling scores 49/100 for bushfire risk. Stirling Range National Park scores 53/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Stirling or Stirling Range National Park?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Stirling Range National Park has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.
